chapters.en: We remember the fish, which we did eat in Egypt freely; the cucumbers, and the melons, and the leeks, and the onions, and the garlick:

chapters.zh: 现在我们的心血枯竭了，除这吗哪以外，在我们眼前并没有别的东西。
chapters.en: But now our soul dried away: nothing at all, beside this manna, our eyes.

chapters.zh: 这吗哪彷佛芫荽子，又好像珍珠。
chapters.en: And the manna as coriander seed, and the colour thereof as the colour of bdellium.

chapters.zh: 百姓周围行走，把吗哪收起来，或用磨推，或用臼捣，煮在锅中，又做成饼，滋味好像新油。
chapters.en: the people went about, and gathered , and ground in mills, or beat in a mortar, and baked in pans, and made cakes of it: and the taste of it was as the taste of fresh oil.

chapters.zh: 夜间露水降在营中，吗哪也随著降下。
chapters.en: And when the dew fell upon the camp in the night, the manna fell upon it.

chapters.zh: 摩西听见百姓，各在各家的帐棚门口哭号。耶和华的怒气便大发作，摩西就不喜悦。
chapters.en: Then Moses heard the people weep throughout their families, every man in the door of his tent: and the anger of the LORD was kindled greatly; Moses also was displeased.

chapters.zh: 摩西对耶和华说：你为何苦待仆人？我为何不在你眼前蒙恩，竟把这管理百姓的重任加在我身上呢？
chapters.en: And Moses said unto the LORD, Wherefore hast thou afflicted thy servant? and wherefore have I not found favour in thy sight, that thou layest the burden of all this people upon me?

chapters.zh: 这百姓岂是我怀的胎，岂是我生下来的呢？你竟对我说：把他们抱在怀里，如养育之父抱吃奶的孩子，直抱到你起誓应许给他们祖宗的地去。
chapters.en: Have I conceived all this people? have I begotten them, that thou shouldest say unto me, Carry them in thy bosom, as a nursing father beareth the sucking child, unto the land which thou swarest unto their fathers?

chapters.zh: 我从那里得肉给这百姓吃呢？他们都向我哭号说：你给我们肉吃罢！
chapters.en: Whence should I have flesh to give unto all this people? for they weep unto me, saying, Give us flesh, that we may eat.

chapters.zh: 管理这百姓的责任太重了，我独自担当不起。
chapters.en: I am not able to bear all this people alone, because too heavy for me.

chapters.zh: 你这样待我，我若在你眼前蒙恩，求你立时将我杀了，不叫我见自己的苦情。
chapters.en: And if thou deal thus with me, kill me, I pray thee, out of hand, if I have found favour in thy sight; and let me not see my wretchedness.

chapters.zh: 耶和华对摩西说：你从以色列的长老中招聚七十个人，就是你所知道作百姓的长老和官长的，到我这里来，领他们到会幕前，使他们和你一同站立。
chapters.en: And the LORD said unto Moses, Gather unto me seventy men of the elders of Israel, whom thou knowest to be the elders of the people, and officers over them; and bring them unto the tabernacle of the congregation, that they may stand there with thee.

chapters.zh: 我要在那里降临，与你说话，也要把降于你身上的灵分赐他们，他们就和你同当这管百姓的重任，免得你独自担当。
chapters.en: And I will come down and talk with thee there: and I will take of the spirit which upon thee, and will put upon them; and they shall bear the burden of the people with thee, that thou bear not thyself alone.

chapters.zh: 又要对百姓说：你们应当自洁，预备明天吃肉，因为你们哭号说：谁给我们肉吃！我们在埃及很好。这声音达到了耶和华的耳中，所以他必给你们肉吃。
chapters.en: And say thou unto the people, Sanctify yourselves against to morrow, and ye shall eat flesh: for ye have wept in the ears of the LORD, saying, Who shall give us flesh to eat? for well with us in Egypt: therefore the LORD will give you flesh, and ye shall eat.

chapters.zh: 你们不止吃一天、两天、五天、十天、二十天，
chapters.en: Ye shall not eat one day, nor two days, nor five days, neither ten days, nor twenty days;

chapters.zh: 要吃一个整月，甚至肉从你们鼻孔里喷出来，使你们厌恶了，因为你们厌弃住在你们中间的耶和华，在他面前哭号说：我们为何出了埃及呢？
chapters.en: even a whole month, until it come out at your nostrils, and it be loathsome unto you: because that ye have despised the LORD which among you, and have wept before him, saying, Why came we forth out of Egypt?

chapters.zh: 摩西对耶和华说：这与我同住的百姓、步行的男人有六十万，你还说：我要把肉给他们，使他们可以吃一个整月。
chapters.en: And Moses said, The people, among whom I , six hundred thousand footmen; and thou hast said, I will give them flesh, that they may eat a whole month.

chapters.zh: 难道给他们宰了羊群牛群，或是把海中所有的鱼都聚了来，就够他们吃么？
chapters.en: Shall the flocks and the herds be slain for them, to suffice them? or shall all the fish of the sea be gathered together for them, to suffice them?

chapters.zh: 耶和华对摩西说：耶和华的膀臂岂是缩短了么？现在要看我的话向你应验不应验。
chapters.en: And the LORD said unto Moses, Is the LORD'S hand waxed short? thou shalt see now whether my word shall come to pass unto thee or not.

chapters.zh: 摩西出去，将耶和华的话告诉百姓，又招聚百姓的长老中七十个人来，使他们站在会幕的四围。
chapters.en: And Moses went out, and told the people the words of the LORD, and gathered the seventy men of the elders of the people, and set them round about the tabernacle.

chapters.zh: 耶和华在云中降临，对摩西说话，把降与他身上的灵分赐那七十个长老。灵停在他们身上的时候，他们就受感说话，以后却没有再说。
chapters.en: And the LORD came down in a cloud, and spake unto him, and took of the spirit that upon him, and gave unto the seventy elders: and it came to pass, , when the spirit rested upon them, they prophesied, and did not cease.

chapters.zh: 但有两个人仍在营里，一个名叫伊利达，一个名叫米达。他们本是在那些被录的人中，却没有到会幕那里去。灵停在他们身上，他们就在营里说预言。
chapters.en: But there remained two men in the camp, the name of the one Eldad, and the name of the other Medad: and the spirit rested upon them; and they of them that were written, but went not out unto the tabernacle: and they prophesied in the camp.

chapters.zh: 有个少年人跑来告诉摩西说：伊利达、米达在营里说预言。
chapters.en: And there ran a young man, and told Moses, and said, Eldad and Medad do prophesy in the camp.

chapters.zh: 摩西的帮手，嫩的儿子约书亚，就是摩西所拣选的一个人，说：请我主摩西禁止他们。
chapters.en: And Joshua the son of Nun, the servant of Moses, of his young men, answered and said, My lord Moses, forbid them.

chapters.zh: 摩西对他说：你为我的缘故嫉妒人么？惟愿耶和华的百姓都受感说话！愿耶和华把他的灵降在他们身上！
chapters.en: And Moses said unto him, Enviest thou for my sake? would God that all the LORD'S people were prophets, that the LORD would put his spirit upon them!

chapters.zh: 于是，摩西和以色列的长老都回到营里去了。
chapters.en: And Moses gat him into the camp, he and the elders of Israel.

chapters.zh: 有风从耶和华那里刮起，把鹌鹑由海面刮来，飞散在营边和营的四围；这边约有一天的路程，那边约有一天的路程，离地面约有二肘。
chapters.en: And there went forth a wind from the LORD, and brought quails from the sea, and let fall by the camp, as it were a day's journey on this side, and as it were a day's journey on the other side, round about the camp, and as it were two cubits upon the face of the earth.

chapters.zh: 百姓起来，终日终夜，并次日一整天，捕取鹌鹑；至少的也取了十贺梅珥，为自己摆列在营的四围。
chapters.en: And the people stood up all that day, and all night, and all the next day, and they gathered the quails: he that gathered least gathered ten homers: and they spread all abroad for themselves round about the camp.

chapters.zh: 肉在他们牙齿之间尚未嚼烂，耶和华的怒气就向他们发作，用最重的灾殃击杀了他们。
chapters.en: And while the flesh yet between their teeth, ere it was chewed, the wrath of the LORD was kindled against the people, and the LORD smote the people with a very great plague.

chapters.zh: 那地方便叫做基博罗哈他瓦（就是贪欲之人的坟墓），因为他们在那里葬埋那起贪欲之心的人。
chapters.en: And he called the name of that place Kibrothhattaavah: because there they buried the people that lusted.

chapters.zh: 百姓从基博罗哈他瓦走到哈洗录，就住在哈洗录。
chapters.en: the people journeyed from Kibrothhattaavah unto Hazeroth; and abode at Hazeroth.

chapters.zh: 摩西娶了古实女子为妻。米利暗和亚伦因他所娶的古实女子，就毁谤他，说：
chapters.en: And Miriam and Aaron spake against Moses because of the Ethiopian woman whom he had married: for he had married an Ethiopian woman.

chapters.zh: 难道耶和华单与摩西说话，不也与我们说话么？这话耶和华听见了。
chapters.en: And they said, Hath the LORD indeed spoken only by Moses? hath he not spoken also by us? And the LORD heard .

chapters.zh: 摩西为人极其谦和，胜过世上的众人。
chapters.en: (Now the man Moses very meek, above all the men which upon the face of the earth.)

chapters.zh: 耶和华忽然对摩西、亚伦、米利暗说：你们三个人都出来，到会幕这里。他们三个人就出来了。
chapters.en: And the LORD spake suddenly unto Moses, and unto Aaron, and unto Miriam, Come out ye three unto the tabernacle of the congregation. And they three came out.

chapters.zh: 耶和华在云柱中降临，站在会幕门口，召亚伦和米利暗，二人就出来了。
chapters.en: And the LORD came down in the pillar of the cloud, and stood the door of the tabernacle, and called Aaron and Miriam: and they both came forth.

chapters.zh: 耶和华说：你们且听我的话：你们中间若有先知，我 ─ 耶和华必在异象中向他显现，在梦中与他说话。
chapters.en: And he said, Hear now my words: If there be a prophet among you, the LORD will make myself known unto him in a vision, will speak unto him in a dream.

chapters.zh: 我的仆人摩西不是这样；他是在我全家尽忠的。
chapters.en: My servant Moses not so, who faithful in all mine house.

chapters.zh: 我要与他面对面说话，乃是明说，不用谜语，并且他必见我的形像。你们毁谤我的仆人摩西，为何不惧怕呢？
chapters.en: With him will I speak mouth to mouth, even apparently, and not in dark speeches; and the similitude of the LORD shall he behold: wherefore then were ye not afraid to speak against my servant Moses?

chapters.zh: 耶和华就向他们二人发怒而去。
chapters.en: And the anger of the LORD was kindled against them; and he departed.

chapters.zh: 云彩从会幕上挪开了，不料，米利暗长了大痲疯，有雪那样白，亚伦一看米利暗长了大痲疯，
chapters.en: And the cloud departed from off the tabernacle; and, behold, Miriam leprous, as snow: and Aaron looked upon Miriam, and, behold, leprous.

chapters.zh: 就对摩西说：我主阿，求你不要因我们愚昧犯罪，便将这罪加在我们身上。
chapters.en: And Aaron said unto Moses, Alas, my lord, I beseech thee, lay not the sin upon us, wherein we have done foolishly, and wherein we have sinned.

chapters.zh: 求你不要使他像那出母腹、肉已半烂的死胎。
chapters.en: Let her not be as one dead, of whom the flesh is half consumed when he cometh out of his mother's womb.

chapters.zh: 于是摩西哀求耶和华说：神阿，求你医治他！
chapters.en: And Moses cried unto the LORD, saying, Heal her now, O God, I beseech thee.

chapters.zh: 耶和华对摩西说：他父亲若吐唾沫在他脸上，他岂不蒙羞七天么？现在要把他在营外关锁七天，然后才可以领他进来。
chapters.en: And the LORD said unto Moses, If her father had but spit in her face, should she not be ashamed seven days? let her be shut out from the camp seven days, and after that let her be received in .

chapters.zh: 于是米利暗关锁在营外七天。百姓没有行路，直等到把米利暗领进来。
chapters.en: And Miriam was shut out from the camp seven days: and the people journeyed not till Miriam was brought in .

chapters.zh: 以后百姓从哈洗录起行，在巴兰的旷野安营。
chapters.en: And afterward the people removed from Hazeroth, and pitched in the wilderness of Paran.

chapters.zh: 耶和华晓谕摩西说：
chapters.en: And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

chapters.zh: 你打发人去窥探我所赐给以色列人的迦南地，他们每支派中要打发一个人，都要作首领的。
chapters.en: Send thou men, that they may search the land of Canaan, which I give unto the children of Israel: of every tribe of their fathers shall ye send a man, every one a ruler among them.

chapters.zh: 摩西就照耶和华的吩咐，从巴兰的旷野打发他们去；他们都是以色列人的族长。
chapters.en: And Moses by the commandment of the LORD sent them from the wilderness of Paran: all those men heads of the children of Israel.

chapters.zh: 他们的名字：属流便支派的有撒刻的儿子沙母亚。
chapters.en: And these their names: of the tribe of Reuben, Shammua the son of Zaccur.

chapters.zh: 属西缅支派的有何利的儿子的沙法。
chapters.en: Of the tribe of Simeon, Shaphat the son of Hori.

chapters.zh: 属犹大支派的有耶孚尼的儿子迦勒。
chapters.en: Of the tribe of Judah, Caleb the son of Jephunneh.

chapters.zh: 属以萨迦支派的有约色的儿子以迦。
chapters.en: Of the tribe of Issachar, Igal the son of Joseph.

chapters.zh: 属以法莲支派的有嫩的儿子何西阿。
chapters.en: Of the tribe of Ephraim, Oshea the son of Nun.

chapters.zh: 属便雅悯支派的有拉孚的儿子帕提。
chapters.en: Of the tribe of Benjamin, Palti the son of Raphu.

chapters.zh: 属西布伦支派的有梭底的儿子迦叠。
chapters.en: Of the tribe of Zebulun, Gaddiel the son of Sodi.

chapters.zh: 约瑟的子孙属玛拿西支派的有稣西的儿子迦底。
chapters.en: Of the tribe of Joseph, , of the tribe of Manasseh, Gaddi the son of Susi.

chapters.zh: 属但支派的有基玛利的儿子亚米利。
chapters.en: Of the tribe of Dan, Ammiel the son of Gemalli.

chapters.zh: 属亚设支派的有米迦勒的儿子西帖。
chapters.en: Of the tribe of Asher, Sethur the son of Michael.

chapters.zh: 属拿弗他利支派的有缚西的儿子拿比。
chapters.en: Of the tribe of Naphtali, Nahbi the son of Vophsi.

chapters.zh: 属迦得支派的有玛基的儿子臼利。
chapters.en: Of the tribe of Gad, Geuel the son of Machi.

chapters.zh: 这就是摩西所打发窥探那地之人的名字。摩西就称嫩的儿子何西阿为约书亚。
chapters.en: These the names of the men which Moses sent to spy out the land. And Moses called Oshea the son of Nun Jehoshua.

chapters.zh: 摩西打发他们去窥探迦南地，说：你们从南地上山地去，
chapters.en: And Moses sent them to spy out the land of Canaan, and said unto them, Get you up this southward, and go up into the mountain:

chapters.zh: 看那地如何，其中所住的民是强是弱，是多是少，
chapters.en: And see the land, what it ; and the people that dwelleth therein, whether they strong or weak, few or many;

chapters.zh: 所住之地是好是歹，所住之处是营盘是坚城。
chapters.en: And what the land that they dwell in, whether it good or bad; and what cities that they dwell in, whether in tents, or in strong holds;

chapters.zh: 又看那地土是肥美是瘠薄，其中有树木没有。你们要放开胆量，把那地的果子带些来。那时正是葡萄初熟的时候。
chapters.en: And what the land , whether it fat or lean, whether there be wood therein, or not. And be ye of good courage, and bring of the fruit of the land. Now the time the time of the firstripe grapes.

chapters.zh: 他们上去窥探那地，从寻的旷野到利合，直到哈马口。
chapters.en: So they went up, and searched the land from the wilderness of Zin unto Rehob, as men come to Hamath.

chapters.zh: 他们从南地上去，到了希伯仑；在那里有亚衲族人亚希幔、示筛、挞买。原来希伯仑城被建造比埃及的锁安城早七年。
chapters.en: And they ascended by the south, and came unto Hebron; where Ahiman, Sheshai, and Talmai, the children of Anak, . (Now Hebron was built seven years before Zoan in Egypt.)

chapters.zh: 他们到了以实各谷，从那里砍了葡萄树的一枝，上头有一挂葡萄，两个人用杠抬著，又带了些石榴和无花果来。
chapters.en: And they came unto the brook of Eshcol, and cut down from thence a branch with one cluster of grapes, and they bare it between two upon a staff; and of the pomegranates, and of the figs.

chapters.zh: 因为以色列人从那里砍来的那挂葡萄，所以那地方叫做以实各谷。
chapters.en: The place was called the brook Eshcol, because of the cluster of grapes which the children of Israel cut down from thence.

chapters.zh: 过了四十天，他们窥探那地才回来，
chapters.en: And they returned from searching of the land after forty days.

chapters.zh: 到了巴兰旷野的加低斯，见摩西、亚伦，并以色列的全会众，回报摩西、亚伦，并全会众，又把那地的果子给他们看；
chapters.en: And they went and came to Moses, and to Aaron, and to all the congregation of the children of Israel, unto the wilderness of Paran, to Kadesh; and brought back word unto them, and unto all the congregation, and shewed them the fruit of the land.

chapters.zh: 又告诉摩西说：我们到了你所打发我们去的那地，果然是流奶与蜜之地；这就是那地的果子。
chapters.en: And they told him, and said, We came unto the land whither thou sentest us, and surely it floweth with milk and honey; and this the fruit of it.

chapters.zh: 然而住那地的民强壮，城邑也坚固宽大，并且我们在那里看见了亚衲族的人。
chapters.en: Nevertheless the people strong that dwell in the land, and the cities walled, very great: and moreover we saw the children of Anak there.

chapters.zh: 亚玛力人住在南地；赫人、耶布斯人、亚摩利人、住在山地；迦南人住在海边并约但河旁。
chapters.en: The Amalekites dwell in the land of the south: and the Hittites, and the Jebusites, and the Amorites, dwell in the mountains: and the Canaanites dwell by the sea, and by the coast of Jordan.

chapters.zh: 迦勒在摩西面前安抚百姓，说：我们立刻上去得那地罢！我们足能得胜。
chapters.en: And Caleb stilled the people before Moses, and said, Let us go up at once, and possess it; for we are well able to overcome it.

chapters.zh: 但那些和他同去的人说：我们不能上去攻击那民，因为他们比我们强壮。
chapters.en: But the men that went up with him said, We be not able to go up against the people; for they stronger than we.

chapters.zh: 探子中有人论到所窥探之地，向以色列人报恶信，说：我们所窥探、经过之地是吞吃居民之地，我们在那里所看见的人民都身量高大。
chapters.en: And they brought up an evil report of the land which they had searched unto the children of Israel, saying, The land, through which we have gone to search it, a land that eateth up the inhabitants thereof; and all the people that we saw in it men of a great stature.

chapters.zh: 我们在那里看见亚衲族人，就是伟人；他们是伟人的后裔。据我们看，自己就如蚱蜢一样；据他们看，我们也是如此。
chapters.en: And there we saw the giants, the sons of Anak, of the giants: and we were in our own sight as grasshoppers, and so we were in their sight.

chapters.zh: 当下，全会众大声喧嚷；那夜百姓都哭号。
chapters.en: And all the congregation lifted up their voice, and cried; and the people wept that night.

chapters.zh: 以色列众人向摩西、亚伦发怨言；全会众对他们说：巴不得我们早死在埃及地，或是死在这旷野。
chapters.en: And all the children of Israel murmured against Moses and against Aaron: and the whole congregation said unto them, Would God that we had died in the land of Egypt! or would God we had died in this wilderness!

chapters.zh: 耶和华为甚么把我们领到那地，使我们倒在刀下呢？我们的妻子和孩子必被掳掠。我们回埃及去岂不好么？
chapters.en: And wherefore hath the LORD brought us unto this land, to fall by the sword, that our wives and our children should be a prey? were it not better for us to return into Egypt?

chapters.zh: 众人彼此说：我们不如立一个首领回埃及去罢！
chapters.en: And they said one to another, Let us make a captain, and let us return into Egypt.

chapters.zh: 摩西、亚伦就俯伏在以色列全会众面前。
chapters.en: Then Moses and Aaron fell on their faces before all the assembly of the congregation of the children of Israel.

chapters.zh: 窥探地的人中，嫩的儿子约书亚和耶孚尼的儿子迦勒撕裂衣服，
chapters.en: And Joshua the son of Nun, and Caleb the son of Jephunneh, of them that searched the land, rent their clothes:

chapters.zh: 对以色列全会众说：我们所窥探、经过之地是极美之地。
chapters.en: And they spake unto all the company of the children of Israel, saying, The land, which we passed through to search it, an exceeding good land.

chapters.zh: 耶和华若喜悦我们，就必将我们领进那地，把地赐给我们；那地原是流奶与蜜之地。
chapters.en: If the LORD delight in us, then he will bring us into this land, and give it us; a land which floweth with milk and honey.

chapters.zh: 但你们不可背叛耶和华，也不要怕那地的居民；因为他们是我们的食物，并且荫庇他们的已经离开他们。有耶和华与我们同在，不要怕他们！
chapters.en: Only rebel not ye against the LORD, neither fear ye the people of the land; for they bread for us: their defence is departed from them, and the LORD with us: fear them not.

chapters.zh: 但全会众说：拿石头打死他们二人。忽然，耶和华的荣光在会幕中向以色列众人显现。
chapters.en: But all the congregation bade stone them with stones. And the glory of the LORD appeared in the tabernacle of the congregation before all the children of Israel.

chapters.zh: 耶和华对摩西说：这百姓藐视我要到几时呢？我在他们中间行了这一切神迹，他们还不信我要到几时呢？
chapters.en: And the LORD said unto Moses, How long will this people provoke me? and how long will it be ere they believe me, for all the signs which I have shewed among them?

chapters.zh: 我要用瘟疫击杀他们，使他们不得承受那地，叫你的后裔成为大国，比他们强胜。
chapters.en: I will smite them with the pestilence, and disinherit them, and will make of thee a greater nation and mightier than they.

chapters.zh: 摩西对耶和华说：埃及人必听见这事；因为你曾施展大能，将这百姓从他们中间领上来。
chapters.en: And Moses said unto the LORD, Then the Egyptians shall hear , (for thou broughtest up this people in thy might from among them;)

chapters.zh: 埃及人要将这事传给迦南地的居民；那民已经听见你 ─ 耶和华是在这百姓中间；因为你面对面被人看见，有你的云彩停在他们以上。你日间在云柱中，夜间在火柱中，在他们前面行。
chapters.en: And they will tell to the inhabitants of this land: they have heard that thou LORD among this people, that thou LORD art seen face to face, and thy cloud standeth over them, and thou goest before them, by day time in a pillar of a cloud, and in a pillar of fire by night.

chapters.zh: 如今你若把这百姓杀了，如杀一人，那些听见你名声的列邦必议论说：
chapters.en: Now thou shalt kill this people as one man, then the nations which have heard the fame of thee will speak, saying,

chapters.zh: 耶和华因为不能把这百姓领进他向他们起誓应许之地，所以在旷野把他们杀了。
chapters.en: Because the LORD was not able to bring this people into the land which he sware unto them, therefore he hath slain them in the wilderness.

chapters.zh: 现在求主大显能力，照你所说过的话说：
chapters.en: And now, I beseech thee, let the power of my Lord be great, according as thou hast spoken, saying,

chapters.zh: 耶和华不轻易发怒，并有丰盛的慈爱，赦免罪孽和过犯；万不以有罪的为无罪，必追讨他的罪，自父及子，直到三、四代。
chapters.en: The LORD longsuffering, and of great mercy, forgiving iniquity and transgression, and by no means clearing , visiting the iniquity of the fathers upon the children unto the third and fourth .

chapters.zh: 求你照你的大慈爱赦免这百姓的罪孽，好像你从埃及到如今常赦免他们一样。
chapters.en: Pardon, I beseech thee, the iniquity of this people according unto the greatness of thy mercy, and as thou hast forgiven this people, from Egypt even until now.

chapters.zh: 耶和华说：我照著你的话赦免了他们。
chapters.en: And the LORD said, I have pardoned according to thy word:

chapters.zh: 然我指著我的永生起誓，遍地要被我的荣耀充满。
chapters.en: But truly I live, all the earth shall be filled with the glory of the LORD.

chapters.zh: 这些人虽看见我的荣耀和我在埃及与旷野所行的神迹，仍然试探我这十次，不听从我的话，
chapters.en: Because all those men which have seen my glory, and my miracles, which I did in Egypt and in the wilderness, and have tempted me now these ten times, and have not hearkened to my voice;

chapters.zh: 他们断不得看见我向他们的祖宗所起誓应许之地。凡藐视我的，一个也不得看见；
chapters.en: Surely they shall not see the land which I sware unto their fathers, neither shall any of them that provoked me see it:

chapters.zh: 惟独我的仆人迦勒，因他另有一个心志，专一跟从我，我就把他领进他所去过的那地；他的后裔也必得那地为业。
chapters.en: But my servant Caleb, because he had another spirit with him, and hath followed me fully, him will I bring into the land whereinto he went; and his seed shall possess it.

chapters.zh: 亚玛力人和迦南人住在谷中，明天你们要转回，从红海的路往旷野去。
chapters.en: (Now the Amalekites and the Canaanites dwelt in the valley.) To morrow turn you, and get you into the wilderness by the way of the Red sea.

chapters.zh: 耶和华对摩西、亚伦说：
chapters.en: And the LORD spake unto Moses and unto Aaron, saying,

chapters.zh: 这恶会众向我发怨言，我忍耐他们要到几时呢？以色列人向我所发的怨言，我都听见了。
chapters.en: How long this evil congregation, which murmur against me? I have heard the murmurings of the children of Israel, which they murmur against me.

chapters.zh: 你们告诉他们，耶和华说：我指著我的永生起誓，我必要照你们达到我耳中的话待你们。
chapters.en: Say unto them, I live, saith the LORD, as ye have spoken in mine ears, so will I do to you:

chapters.zh: 你们的尸首必倒在这旷野，并且你们中间凡被数点、从二十岁以外、向我发怨言的，
chapters.en: Your carcases shall fall in this wilderness; and all that were numbered of you, according to your whole number, from twenty years old and upward, which have murmured against me,

chapters.zh: 必不得进我起誓应许叫你们住的那地；惟有耶孚尼的儿子迦勒和嫩的儿子约书亚才能进去。
chapters.en: Doubtless ye shall not come into the land, which I sware to make you dwell therein, save Caleb the son of Jephunneh, and Joshua the son of Nun.

chapters.zh: 但你们的妇人孩子，就是你们所说、要被掳掠的，我必把他们领进去，他们就得知你们所厌弃的那地。
chapters.en: But your little ones, which ye said should be a prey, them will I bring in, and they shall know the land which ye have despised.

chapters.zh: 至于你们，你们的尸首必倒在这旷野；
chapters.en: But you, your carcases, they shall fall in this wilderness.

chapters.zh: 你们的儿女必在旷野飘流四十年，担当你们淫行的罪，直到你们的尸首在旷野消灭。
chapters.en: And your children shall wander in the wilderness forty years, and bear your whoredoms, until your carcases be wasted in the wilderness.

chapters.zh: 按你们窥探那地的四十日，一年顶一日，你们要担当罪孽四十年，就知道我与你们疏远了，
chapters.en: After the number of the days in which ye searched the land, forty days, each day for a year, shall ye bear your iniquities, forty years, and ye shall know my breach of promise.

chapters.zh: 我 ─ 耶和华说过，我总要这样待这一切聚集敌我的恶会众；他们必在这旷野消灭，在这里死亡。
chapters.en: I the LORD have said, I will surely do it unto all this evil congregation, that are gathered together against me: in this wilderness they shall be consumed, and there they shall die.

chapters.zh: 摩西所打发、窥探那地的人回来，报那地的恶信，叫全会众向摩西发怨言，
chapters.en: And the men, which Moses sent to search the land, who returned, and made all the congregation to murmur against him, by bringing up a slander upon the land,

chapters.zh: 这些报恶信的人都遭瘟疫，死在耶和华面前。
chapters.en: Even those men that did bring up the evil report upon the land, died by the plague before the LORD.

chapters.zh: 其中惟有嫩的儿子约书亚和耶孚尼的儿子迦勒仍然存活。
chapters.en: But Joshua the son of Nun, and Caleb the son of Jephunneh, of the men that went to search the land, lived .

chapters.zh: 摩西将这些话告诉以色列众人，他们就甚悲哀。
chapters.en: And Moses told these sayings unto all the children of Israel: and the people mourned greatly.

chapters.zh: 清早起来，上山顶去，说：我们在这里，我们有罪了；情愿上耶和华所应许的地方去。
chapters.en: And they rose up early in the morning, and gat them up into the top of the mountain, saying, Lo, we , and will go up unto the place which the LORD hath promised: for we have sinned.

chapters.zh: 摩西说：你们为何违背耶和华的命令呢？这事不能顺利了。
chapters.en: And Moses said, Wherefore now do ye transgress the commandment of the LORD? but it shall not prosper.

chapters.zh: 不要上去；因为耶和华不在你们中间，恐怕你们被仇敌杀败了。
chapters.en: Go not up, for the LORD not among you; that ye be not smitten before your enemies.

chapters.zh: 亚玛力人和迦南人都在你们面前，你们必倒在刀下；因你们退回不跟从耶和华，所以他必不与你们同在。
chapters.en: For the Amalekites and the Canaanites there before you, and ye shall fall by the sword: because ye are turned away from the LORD, therefore the LORD will not be with you.

chapters.zh: 他们却擅敢上山顶去，然而耶和华的约柜和摩西没有出营。
chapters.en: But they presumed to go up unto the hill top: nevertheless the ark of the covenant of the LORD, and Moses, departed not out of the camp.

chapters.zh: 于是亚玛力人和住在那山上的迦南人都下来击打他们，把他们杀退了，直到何珥玛。
chapters.en: Then the Amalekites came down, and the Canaanites which dwelt in that hill, and smote them, and discomfited them, unto Hormah.

chapters.zh: 耶和华对摩西说：
chapters.en: And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

chapters.zh: 你晓谕以色列人说：你们到了我所赐给你们居住的地，
chapters.en: Speak unto the children of Israel, and say unto them, When ye be come into the land of your habitations, which I give unto you,

chapters.zh: 若愿意从牛群羊群中取牛羊作火祭，献给耶和华，无论是燔祭是平安祭，为要还特许的愿，或是作甘心祭，或是逢你们节期献的，都要奉给耶和华为馨香之祭。
chapters.en: And will make an offering by fire unto the LORD, a burnt offering, or a sacrifice in performing a vow, or in a freewill offering, or in your solemn feasts, to make a sweet savour unto the LORD, of the herd, or of the flock:

chapters.zh: 那献供物的就要将细面伊法十分之一，并油一欣四分之一，调和作素祭，献给耶和华。
chapters.en: Then shall he that offereth his offering unto the LORD bring a meat offering of a tenth deal of flour mingled with the fourth of an hin of oil.

chapters.zh: 无论是燔祭是平安祭，你要为每只绵羊羔，一同预备奠祭的酒一欣四分之一。
chapters.en: And the fourth of an hin of wine for a drink offering shalt thou prepare with the burnt offering or sacrifice, for one lamb.

chapters.zh: 为公绵羊预备细面伊法十分之二，并油一欣三分之一，调和作素祭，
chapters.en: Or for a ram, thou shalt prepare a meat offering two tenth deals of flour mingled with the third of an hin of oil.

chapters.zh: 又用酒一欣三分之一作奠祭，献给耶和华为馨香之祭。
chapters.en: And for a drink offering thou shalt offer the third of an hin of wine, a sweet savour unto the LORD.

chapters.zh: 你预备公牛作燔祭，或是作平安祭，为要还特许的愿，或是作平安祭，献给耶和华，
chapters.en: And when thou preparest a bullock a burnt offering, or a sacrifice in performing a vow, or peace offerings unto the LORD:

chapters.zh: 就要把细面伊法十分之三，并油半欣，调和作素祭，和公牛一同献上，
chapters.en: Then shall he bring with a bullock a meat offering of three tenth deals of flour mingled with half an hin of oil.

chapters.zh: 又用酒半欣作奠祭，献给耶和华为馨香的火祭。
chapters.en: And thou shalt bring for a drink offering half an hin of wine, an offering made by fire, of a sweet savour unto the LORD.

chapters.zh: 献公牛、公绵羊、绵羊羔、山羊羔，每只都要这样办理。
chapters.en: Thus shall it be done for one bullock, or for one ram, or for a lamb, or a kid.

chapters.zh: 照你们所预备的数目，按著只数都要这样办理。
chapters.en: According to the number that ye shall prepare, so shall ye do to every one according to their number.

chapters.zh: 凡本地人将馨香的火祭献给耶和华，都要这样办理。
chapters.en: All that are born of the country shall do these things after this manner, in offering an offering made by fire, of a sweet savour unto the LORD.

chapters.zh: 若有外人和你们同居，或有人世世代代住在你们中间，愿意将馨香的火祭献给耶和华，你们怎样办理，他也要照样办理。
chapters.en: And if a stranger sojourn with you, or whosoever among you in your generations, and will offer an offering made by fire, of a sweet savour unto the LORD; as ye do, so he shall do.

chapters.zh: 至于会众，你们和同居的外人都归一例，作为你们世世代代永远的定例，在耶和华面前，你们怎样，寄居的也要怎样。
chapters.en: One ordinance for you of the congregation, and also for the stranger that sojourneth , an ordinance for ever in your generations: as ye , so shall the stranger be before the LORD.

chapters.zh: 你们并与你们同居的外人当有一样的条例，一样的典章。
chapters.en: One law and one manner shall be for you, and for the stranger that sojourneth with you.

chapters.zh: 耶和华对摩西说：
chapters.en: And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

chapters.zh: 你晓谕以色列人说：你们到了我所领你们进去的那地，
chapters.en: Speak unto the children of Israel, and say unto them, When ye come into the land whither I bring you,

chapters.zh: 吃那地的粮食，就要把举祭献给耶和华。
chapters.en: Then it shall be, that, when ye eat of the bread of the land, ye shall offer up an heave offering unto the LORD.

chapters.zh: 你们要用初熟的麦子磨面，做饼当举祭奉献；你们举上，好像举禾场的举祭一样，
chapters.en: Ye shall offer up a cake of the first of your dough an heave offering: as the heave offering of the threshingfloor, so shall ye heave it.

chapters.zh: 你们世世代代要用初熟的麦子磨面，当举祭献给耶和华。
chapters.en: Of the first of your dough ye shall give unto the LORD an heave offering in your generations.

chapters.zh: 你们有错误的时候，不守耶和华所晓谕摩西的这一切命令，
chapters.en: And if ye have erred, and not observed all these commandments, which the LORD hath spoken unto Moses,

chapters.zh: 就是耶和华藉摩西一切所吩咐你们的，自那日以至你们的世世代代，
chapters.en: all that the LORD hath commanded you by the hand of Moses, from the day that the LORD commanded , and henceforward among your generations;

chapters.zh: 若有误行，是会众所不知道的，后来全会众就要将一只公牛犊作燔祭，并照典章把素祭和奠祭一同献给耶和华为馨香之祭，又献一只公山羊作赎罪祭。
chapters.en: Then it shall be, if be committed by ignorance without the knowledge of the congregation, that all the congregation shall offer one young bullock for a burnt offering, for a sweet savour unto the LORD, with his meat offering, and his drink offering, according to the manner, and one kid of the goats for a sin offering.

chapters.zh: 祭司要为以色列全会众赎罪，他们就必蒙赦免，因为这是错误。他们又因自己的错误，把供物，就是向耶和华献的火祭和赎罪祭，一并奉到耶和华面前。
chapters.en: And the priest shall make an atonement for all the congregation of the children of Israel, and it shall be forgiven them; for it ignorance: and they shall bring their offering, a sacrifice made by fire unto the LORD, and their sin offering before the LORD, for their ignorance:

chapters.zh: 以色列全会众和寄居在他们中间的外人就必蒙赦免，因为这罪是百姓误犯的。
chapters.en: And it shall be forgiven all the congregation of the children of Israel, and the stranger that sojourneth among them; seeing all the people in ignorance.

chapters.zh: 若有一个人误犯了罪，他就要献一岁的母山羊作赎罪祭。
chapters.en: And if any soul sin through ignorance, then he shall bring a she goat of the first year for a sin offering.

chapters.zh: 那误行的人犯罪的时候，祭司要在耶和华面前为他赎罪，他就必蒙赦免。
chapters.en: And the priest shall make an atonement for the soul that sinneth ignorantly, when he sinneth by ignorance before the LORD, to make an atonement for him; and it shall be forgiven him.

chapters.zh: 以色列中的本地人和寄居在他们中间的外人，若误行了甚么事，必归一样的条例，
chapters.en: Ye shall have one law for him that sinneth through ignorance, him that is born among the children of Israel, and for the stranger that sojourneth among them.

chapters.zh: 但那擅敢行事的，无论是本地人是寄居的，他亵渎了耶和华，必从民中剪除。
chapters.en: But the soul that doeth presumptuously, born in the land, or a stranger, the same reproacheth the LORD; and that soul shall be cut off from among his people.

chapters.zh: 因他藐视耶和华的言语，违背耶和华的命令，那人总要剪除；他的罪孽要归到他身上。
chapters.en: Because he hath despised the word of the LORD, and hath broken his commandment, that soul shall utterly be cut off; his iniquity upon him.

chapters.zh: 以色列人在旷野的时候，遇见一个人在安息日捡柴。
chapters.en: And while the children of Israel were in the wilderness, they found a man that gathered sticks upon the sabbath day.

chapters.zh: 遇见他捡柴的人，就把他带到摩西、亚伦并全会众那里，
chapters.en: And they that found him gathering sticks brought him unto Moses and Aaron, and unto all the congregation.

chapters.zh: 将他收在监内；因为当怎样办他，还没有指明。
chapters.en: And they put him in ward, because it was not declared what should be done to him.

chapters.zh: 耶和华吩咐摩西说：总要把那人治死；全会众要在营外用石头把他打死。
chapters.en: And the LORD said unto Moses, The man shall be surely put to death: all the congregation shall stone him with stones without the camp.

chapters.zh: 于是全会众将他带到营外，用石头打死他，是照耶和华所吩咐摩西的。
chapters.en: And all the congregation brought him without the camp, and stoned him with stones, and he died; as the LORD commanded Moses.

chapters.zh: 耶和华晓谕摩西说：
chapters.en: And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

chapters.zh: 你吩咐以色列人，叫他们世世代代在衣服边上做 䍁 子，又在底边的 䍁 子上钉一根蓝细带子。
chapters.en: Speak unto the children of Israel, and bid them that they make them fringes in the borders of their garments throughout their generations, and that they put upon the fringe of the borders a ribband of blue:

chapters.zh: 你们佩带这 䍁 子，好叫你们看见就记念遵行耶和华一切的命令，不随从自己的心意、眼目行邪淫，像你们素常一样；
chapters.en: And it shall be unto you for a fringe, that ye may look upon it, and remember all the commandments of the LORD, and do them; and that ye seek not after your own heart and your own eyes, after which ye use to go a whoring:

chapters.zh: 使你们记念遵行我一切的命令，成为圣洁，归与你们的神。
chapters.en: That ye may remember, and do all my commandments, and be holy unto your God.

chapters.zh: 我是耶和华 ─ 你们的神，曾把你们从埃及地领出来，要作你们的神。我是耶和华 ─ 你们的神。
chapters.en: I the LORD your God, which brought you out of the land of Egypt, to be your God: I the LORD your God.

chapters.zh: 利未的曾孙、哥辖的孙子、以斯哈的儿子可拉，和流便子孙中以利押的儿子大坍、亚比兰，与比勒的儿子安，
chapters.en: Now Korah, the son of Izhar, the son of Kohath, the son of Levi, and Dathan and Abiram, the sons of Eliab, and On, the son of Peleth, sons of Reuben, took :

chapters.zh: 并以色列会中的二百五十个首领，就是有名望选入会中的人，在摩西面前一同起来，
chapters.en: And they rose up before Moses, with certain of the children of Israel, two hundred and fifty princes of the assembly, famous in the congregation, men of renown:

chapters.zh: 聚集攻击摩西、亚伦，说：你们擅自专权！全会众个个既是圣洁，耶和华也在他们中间，你们为甚么自高，超过耶和华的会众呢？
chapters.en: And they gathered themselves together against Moses and against Aaron, and said unto them, too much upon you, seeing all the congregation holy, every one of them, and the LORD among them: wherefore then lift ye up yourselves above the congregation of the LORD?

chapters.zh: 摩西听见这话就俯伏在地，
chapters.en: And when Moses heard , he fell upon his face:

chapters.zh: 对可拉和他一党的人说：到了早晨，耶和华必指示谁是属他的，谁是圣洁的，就叫谁亲近他；他所拣选的是谁，必叫谁亲近他。
chapters.en: And he spake unto Korah and unto all his company, saying, Even to morrow the LORD will shew who his, and holy; and will cause to come near unto him: even whom he hath chosen will he cause to come near unto him.

chapters.zh: 可拉阿，你们要这样行，你和你的一党要拿香炉来。
chapters.en: This do; Take you censers, Korah, and all his company;

chapters.zh: 明日，在耶和华面前，把火盛在炉中，把香放在其上。耶和华拣选谁，谁就为圣洁。你们这利未的子孙擅自专权了！
chapters.en: And put fire therein, and put incense in them before the LORD to morrow: and it shall be the man whom the LORD doth choose, he holy: too much upon you, ye sons of Levi.

chapters.zh: 摩西又对可拉说：利未的子孙哪，你们听我说！
chapters.en: And Moses said unto Korah, Hear, I pray you, ye sons of Levi:

chapters.zh: 以色列的神从以色列会中将你们分别出来，使你们亲近他，办耶和华帐幕的事，并站在会众面前替他们当差。
chapters.en: a small thing unto you, that the God of Israel hath separated you from the congregation of Israel, to bring you near to himself to do the service of the tabernacle of the LORD, and to stand before the congregation to minister unto them?

chapters.zh: 耶和华又使你和你一切弟兄 ─ 利未的子孙 ─ 一同亲近他，这岂为小事？你们还要求祭司的职任么？
chapters.en: And he hath brought thee near , and all thy brethren the sons of Levi with thee: and seek ye the priesthood also?

chapters.zh: 你和你一党的人聚集是要攻击耶和华。亚伦算甚么，你们竟向他发怨言呢？
chapters.en: For which cause thou and all thy company gathered together against the LORD: and what Aaron, that ye murmur against him?

chapters.zh: 摩西打发人去召以利押的儿子大坍、亚比兰。他们说：我们不上去！
chapters.en: And Moses sent to call Dathan and Abiram, the sons of Eliab: which said, We will not come up:

chapters.zh: 你将我们从流奶与蜜之地领上来，要在旷野杀我们，这岂为小事？你还要自立为王辖管我们么？
chapters.en: a small thing that thou hast brought us up out of a land that floweth with milk and honey, to kill us in the wilderness, except thou make thyself altogether a prince over us?

chapters.zh: 并且你没有将我们领到流奶与蜜之地，也没有把田地和葡萄园给我们为业。难道你要剜这些人的眼睛么？我们不上去！
chapters.en: Moreover thou hast not brought us into a land that floweth with milk and honey, or given us inheritance of fields and vineyards: wilt thou put out the eyes of these men? we will not come up.

chapters.zh: 摩西就甚发怒，对耶和华说：求你不要享受他们的供物。我并没有夺过他们一匹驴，也没有害过他们一个人。
chapters.en: And Moses was very wroth, and said unto the LORD, Respect not thou their offering: I have not taken one ass from them, neither have I hurt one of them.

chapters.zh: 摩西对可拉说：明天，你和你一党的人，并亚伦，都要站在耶和华面前；
chapters.en: And Moses said unto Korah, Be thou and all thy company before the LORD, thou, and they, and Aaron, to morrow:

chapters.zh: 各人要拿一个香炉，共二百五十个，把香放在上面，到耶和华面前。你和亚伦也各拿自己的香炉。
chapters.en: And take every man his censer, and put incense in them, and bring ye before the LORD every man his censer, two hundred and fifty censers; thou also, and Aaron, each his censer.

chapters.zh: 于是他们各人拿一个香炉，盛上火，加上香，同摩西、亚伦站在会幕门前。
chapters.en: And they took every man his censer, and put fire in them, and laid incense thereon, and stood in the door of the tabernacle of the congregation with Moses and Aaron.

chapters.zh: 可拉招聚全会众到会幕门前，要攻击摩西、亚伦；耶和华的荣光就向全会众显现。
chapters.en: And Korah gathered all the congregation against them unto the door of the tabernacle of the congregation: and the glory of the LORD appeared unto all the congregation.

chapters.zh: 耶和华晓谕摩西、亚伦说：
chapters.en: And the LORD spake unto Moses and unto Aaron, saying,

chapters.zh: 你们离开这会众，我好在转眼之间把他们灭绝。
chapters.en: Separate yourselves from among this congregation, that I may consume them in a moment.

chapters.zh: 摩西、亚伦就俯伏在地，说：神，万人之灵的神阿，一人犯罪，你就要向全会众发怒么？
chapters.en: And they fell upon their faces, and said, O God, the God of the spirits of all flesh, shall one man sin, and wilt thou be wroth with all the congregation?

chapters.zh: 耶和华晓谕摩西说：
chapters.en: And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

chapters.zh: 你吩咐会众说：你们离开可拉、大坍、亚比兰帐棚的四围。
chapters.en: Speak unto the congregation, saying, Get you up from about the tabernacle of Korah, Dathan, and Abiram.

chapters.zh: 摩西起来，往大坍、亚比兰那里去；以色列的长老也随著他去。
chapters.en: And Moses rose up and went unto Dathan and Abiram; and the elders of Israel followed him.

chapters.zh: 他吩咐会众说：你们离开这恶人的帐棚罢，他们的物件，甚么都不可摸，恐怕你们陷在他们的罪中，与他们一同消灭。
chapters.en: And he spake unto the congregation, saying, Depart, I pray you, from the tents of these wicked men, and touch nothing of theirs, lest ye be consumed in all their sins.

chapters.zh: 于是众人离开可拉、大坍、亚比兰帐棚的四围。大坍、亚比兰带著妻子、儿女、小孩子，都出来，站在自己的帐棚门口。
chapters.en: So they gat up from the tabernacle of Korah, Dathan, and Abiram, on every side: and Dathan and Abiram came out, and stood in the door of their tents, and their wives, and their sons, and their little children.

chapters.zh: 摩西说：我行的这一切事本不是凭我自己心意行的，乃是耶和华打发我行的，必有证据使你们知道。
chapters.en: And Moses said, Hereby ye shall know that the LORD hath sent me to do all these works; for not of mine own mind.

chapters.zh: 这些人死若与世人无异，或是他们所遭的与世人相同，就不是耶和华打发我来的。
chapters.en: If these men die the common death of all men, or if they be visited after the visitation of all men; the LORD hath not sent me.

chapters.zh: 倘若耶和华创作一件新事，使地开口，把他们和一切属他们的都吞下去，叫他们活活的坠落阴间，你们就明白这些人是藐视耶和华了。
chapters.en: But if the LORD make a new thing, and the earth open her mouth, and swallow them up, with all that unto them, and they go down quick into the pit; then ye shall understand that these men have provoked the LORD.

chapters.zh: 摩西刚说完了这一切话，他们脚下的地就开了口，
chapters.en: And it came to pass, as he had made an end of speaking all these words, that the ground clave asunder that under them:

chapters.zh: 把他们和他们的家眷，并一切属可拉的人丁、财物，都吞下去。
chapters.en: And the earth opened her mouth, and swallowed them up, and their houses, and all the men that unto Korah, and all goods.

chapters.zh: 这样，他们和一切属他们的，都活活的坠落阴间；地口在他们上头照旧合闭，他们就从会中灭亡。
chapters.en: They, and all that to them, went down alive into the pit, and the earth closed upon them: and they perished from among the congregation.

chapters.zh: 在他们四围的以色列众人听他们呼号，就都逃跑，说：恐怕地也把我们吞下去。
chapters.en: And all Israel that round about them fled at the cry of them: for they said, Lest the earth swallow us up .

chapters.zh: 又有火从耶和华那里出来，烧灭了那献香的二百五十个人。
chapters.en: And there came out a fire from the LORD, and consumed the two hundred and fifty men that offered incense.

chapters.zh: 耶和华晓谕摩西说：
chapters.en: And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

chapters.zh: 你吩咐祭司亚伦的儿子以利亚撒从火中捡起那些香炉来，把火撒在别处，因为那些香炉是圣的。
chapters.en: Speak unto Eleazar the son of Aaron the priest, that he take up the censers out of the burning, and scatter thou the fire yonder; for they are hallowed.

chapters.zh: 把那些犯罪、自害己命之人的香炉，叫人锤成片子，用以包坛。那些香炉本是他们在耶和华面前献过的，所以是圣的，并且可以给以色列人作记号。
chapters.en: The censers of these sinners against their own souls, let them make them broad plates a covering of the altar: for they offered them before the LORD, therefore they are hallowed: and they shall be a sign unto the children of Israel.

chapters.zh: 于是祭司以利亚撒将被烧之人所献的铜香炉拿来，人就锤出来，用以包坛，
chapters.en: And Eleazar the priest took the brasen censers, wherewith they that were burnt had offered; and they were made broad a covering of the altar:

chapters.zh: 给以色列人作纪念，使亚伦后裔之外的人不得近前来在耶和华面前烧香，免得他遭可拉和他一党所遭的。这乃是照耶和华藉著摩西所吩咐的。
chapters.en: a memorial unto the children of Israel, that no stranger, which not of the seed of Aaron, come near to offer incense before the LORD; that he be not as Korah, and as his company: as the LORD said to him by the hand of Moses.

chapters.zh: 第二天，以色列全会众都向摩西、亚伦发怨言说：你们杀了耶和华的百姓了。
chapters.en: But on the morrow all the congregation of the children of Israel murmured against Moses and against Aaron, saying, Ye have killed the people of the LORD.

chapters.zh: 会众聚集攻击摩西、亚伦的时候，向会幕观看，不料，有云彩遮盖了，耶和华的荣光显现。
chapters.en: And it came to pass, when the congregation was gathered against Moses and against Aaron, that they looked toward the tabernacle of the congregation: and, behold, the cloud covered it, and the glory of the LORD appeared.

chapters.zh: 摩西、亚伦就来到会幕前。
chapters.en: And Moses and Aaron came before the tabernacle of the congregation.

chapters.zh: 耶和华吩咐摩西说：
chapters.en: And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

chapters.zh: 你们离开这会众，我好在转眼之间把他们灭绝。他们二人就俯伏于地。
chapters.en: Get you up from among this congregation, that I may consume them as in a moment. And they fell upon their faces.

chapters.zh: 摩西对亚伦说：拿你的香炉，把坛上的火盛在其中，又加上香，快快带到会众那里，为他们赎罪；因为有忿怒从耶和华那里出来，瘟疫已经发作了。
chapters.en: And Moses said unto Aaron, Take a censer, and put fire therein from off the altar, and put on incense, and go quickly unto the congregation, and make an atonement for them: for there is wrath gone out from the LORD; the plague is begun.

chapters.zh: 亚伦照著摩西所说的拿来，跑到会中，不料，瘟疫在百姓中已经发作了。他就加上香，为百姓赎罪。
chapters.en: And Aaron took as Moses commanded, and ran into the midst of the congregation; and, behold, the plague was begun among the people: and he put on incense, and made an atonement for the people.

chapters.zh: 他站在活人死人中间，瘟疫就止住了。
chapters.en: And he stood between the dead and the living; and the plague was stayed.

chapters.zh: 除了因可拉事情死的以外，遭瘟疫死的，共有一万四千七百人。
chapters.en: Now they that died in the plague were fourteen thousand and seven hundred, beside them that died about the matter of Korah.

chapters.zh: 亚伦回到会幕门口，到摩西那里，瘟疫已经止住了。
chapters.en: And Aaron returned unto Moses unto the door of the tabernacle of the congregation: and the plague was stayed.

chapters.zh: 耶和华对摩西说：
chapters.en: And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

chapters.zh: 你晓谕以色列人，从他们手下取杖，每支派一根；从他们所有的首领，按著支派，共取十二根。你要将各人的名字写在各人的杖上，
chapters.en: Speak unto the children of Israel, and take of every one of them a rod according to the house of fathers, of all their princes according to the house of their fathers twelve rods: write thou every man's name upon his rod.

chapters.zh: 并要将亚伦的名字写在利未的杖上，因为各族长必有一根杖。
chapters.en: And thou shalt write Aaron's name upon the rod of Levi: for one rod for the head of the house of their fathers.

chapters.zh: 你要把这些杖存在会幕内法柜前，就是我与你们相会之处。
chapters.en: And thou shalt lay them up in the tabernacle of the congregation before the testimony, where I will meet with you.

chapters.zh: 后来我所拣选的那人，他的杖必发芽。这样，我必使以色列人向你们所发的怨言止息，不再达到我耳中。
chapters.en: And it shall come to pass, the man's rod, whom I shall choose, shall blossom: and I will make to cease from me the murmurings of the children of Israel, whereby they murmur against you.

chapters.zh: 于是摩西晓谕以色列人，他们的首领就把杖交给他，按著支派，每首领一根，共有十二根；亚伦的杖也在其中。
chapters.en: And Moses spake unto the children of Israel, and every one of their princes gave him a rod apiece, for each prince one, according to their fathers' houses, twelve rods: and the rod of Aaron among their rods.

chapters.zh: 摩西就把杖存在法柜的帐幕内，在耶和华面前。
chapters.en: And Moses laid up the rods before the LORD in the tabernacle of witness.

chapters.zh: 第二天，摩西进法柜的帐幕去。谁知利未族亚伦的杖已经发了芽，生了花苞，开了花，结了熟杏。
chapters.en: And it came to pass, that on the morrow Moses went into the tabernacle of witness; and, behold, the rod of Aaron for the house of Levi was budded, and brought forth buds, and bloomed blossoms, and yielded almonds.

chapters.zh: 摩西就把所有的杖从耶和华面前拿出来，给以色列众人看；他们看见了，各首领就把自己的杖拿去。
chapters.en: And Moses brought out all the rods from before the LORD unto all the children of Israel: and they looked, and took every man his rod.

chapters.zh: 耶和华吩咐摩西说：把亚伦的杖还放在法柜前，给这些背叛之子留作记号。这样，你就使他们向我发的怨言止息，免得他们死亡。
chapters.en: And the LORD said unto Moses, Bring Aaron's rod again before the testimony, to be kept for a token against the rebels; and thou shalt quite take away their murmurings from me, that they die not.

chapters.zh: 摩西就这样行。耶和华怎样吩咐他，他就怎样行了。
chapters.en: And Moses did : as the LORD commanded him, so did he.

chapters.zh: 以色列人对摩西说：我们死喇！我们灭亡喇！都灭亡喇！
chapters.en: And the children of Israel spake unto Moses, saying, Behold, we die, we perish, we all perish.

chapters.zh: 凡挨近耶和华帐幕的是必死的。我们都要死亡么？
chapters.en: Whosoever cometh any thing near unto the tabernacle of the LORD shall die: shall we be consumed with dying?

chapters.zh: 耶和华对亚伦说：你和你的儿子，并你本族的人，要一同担当干犯圣所的罪孽。你和你的儿子也要一同担当干犯祭司职任的罪孽。
chapters.en: And the LORD said unto Aaron, Thou and thy sons and thy father's house with thee shall bear the iniquity of the sanctuary: and thou and thy sons with thee shall bear the iniquity of your priesthood.

chapters.zh: 你要带你弟兄利未人，就是你祖宗支派的人前来，使他们与你联合，服事你，只是你和你的儿子，要一同在法柜的帐幕前供职。
chapters.en: And thy brethren also of the tribe of Levi, the tribe of thy father, bring thou with thee, that they may be joined unto thee, and minister unto thee: but thou and thy sons with thee before the tabernacle of witness.

chapters.zh: 他们要守所吩咐你的，并守全帐幕，只是不可挨近圣所的器具和坛，免得他们和你们都死亡。
chapters.en: And they shall keep thy charge, and the charge of all the tabernacle: only they shall not come nigh the vessels of the sanctuary and the altar, that neither they, nor ye also, die.

chapters.zh: 他们要与你联合，也要看守会幕，办理帐幕一切的事，只是外人不可挨近你们。
chapters.en: And they shall be joined unto thee, and keep the charge of the tabernacle of the congregation, for all the service of the tabernacle: and a stranger shall not come nigh unto you.

chapters.zh: 你们要看守圣所和坛，免得忿怒再临到以色列人。
chapters.en: And ye shall keep the charge of the sanctuary, and the charge of the altar: that there be no wrath any more upon the children of Israel.

chapters.zh: 我已将你们的弟兄利未人从以色列人中拣选出来归耶和华，是给你们为赏赐的，为要办理会幕的事。
chapters.en: And I, behold, I have taken your brethren the Levites from among the children of Israel: to you given a gift for the LORD, to do the service of the tabernacle of the congregation.

chapters.zh: 你和你的儿子要为一切属坛和幔子内的事一同守祭司的职任。你们要这样供职；我将祭司的职任给你们当作赏赐事奉我。凡挨近的外人必被治死。
chapters.en: Therefore thou and thy sons with thee shall keep your priest's office for every thing of the altar, and within the vail; and ye shall serve: I have given your priest's office as a service of gift: and the stranger that cometh nigh shall be put to death.

chapters.zh: 耶和华晓谕亚伦说：我已将归我的举祭，就是以色列人一切分别为圣的物，交给你经管；因你受过膏，把这些都赐给你和你的子孙，当作永得的分。
chapters.en: And the LORD spake unto Aaron, Behold, I also have given thee the charge of mine heave offerings of all the hallowed things of the children of Israel; unto thee have I given them by reason of the anointing, and to thy sons, by an ordinance for ever.

chapters.zh: 以色列人归给我至圣的供物，就是一切的素祭、赎罪祭、赎愆祭，其中所有存留不经火的，都为至圣之物，要归给你和你的子孙。
chapters.en: This shall be thine of the most holy things, from the fire: every oblation of theirs, every meat offering of theirs, and every sin offering of theirs, and every trespass offering of theirs, which they shall render unto me, most holy for thee and for thy sons.

chapters.zh: 你要拿这些当至圣物吃；凡男丁都可以吃。你当以此物为圣。
chapters.en: In the most holy shalt thou eat it; every male shall eat it: it shall be holy unto thee.

chapters.zh: 以色列人所献的举祭并摇祭都是你的；我已赐给你和你的儿女，当作永得的分；凡在你家中的洁净人都可以吃。
chapters.en: And this thine; the heave offering of their gift, with all the wave offerings of the children of Israel: I have given them unto thee, and to thy sons and to thy daughters with thee, by a statute for ever: every one that is clean in thy house shall eat of it.

chapters.zh: 凡油中、新酒中、五谷中至好的，就是以色列人所献给耶和华初熟之物，我都赐给你。
chapters.en: All the best of the oil, and all the best of the wine, and of the wheat, the firstfruits of them which they shall offer unto the LORD, them have I given thee.

chapters.zh: 凡从他们地上所带来给耶和华初熟之物也都要归与你。你家中的洁净人都可以吃。
chapters.en: whatsoever is first ripe in the land, which they shall bring unto the LORD, shall be thine; every one that is clean in thine house shall eat it.

chapters.zh: 以色列中一切永献的都必归与你。
chapters.en: Every thing devoted in Israel shall be thine.

chapters.zh: 他们所有奉给耶和华的，连人带牲畜，凡头生的，都要归给你；只是人头生的，总要赎出来；不洁净牲畜头生的，也要赎出来。
chapters.en: Every thing that openeth the matrix in all flesh, which they bring unto the LORD, of men or beasts, shall be thine: nevertheless the firstborn of man shalt thou surely redeem, and the firstling of unclean beasts shalt thou redeem.

chapters.zh: 其中在一月之外所当赎的，要照你所估定的价，按圣所的平，用银子五舍客勒赎出来（一舍客勒是二十季拉）。
chapters.en: And those that are to be redeemed from a month old shalt thou redeem, according to thine estimation, for the money of five shekels, after the shekel of the sanctuary, which twenty gerahs.

chapters.zh: 只是头生的牛，或是头生的绵羊和山羊，必不可赎，都是圣的，要把他的血洒在坛上，把他的脂油焚烧，当作馨香的火祭献给耶和华。
chapters.en: But the firstling of a cow, or the firstling of a sheep, or the firstling of a goat, thou shalt not redeem; they holy: thou shalt sprinkle their blood upon the altar, and shalt burn their fat an offering made by fire, for a sweet savour unto the LORD.

chapters.zh: 他的肉必归你，像被摇的胸、被举的右腿归你一样。
chapters.en: And the flesh of them shall be thine, as the wave breast and as the right shoulder are thine.

chapters.zh: 凡以色列人所献给耶和华圣物中的举祭，我都赐给你和你的儿女，当作永得的分。这是给你和你的后裔、在耶和华面前作为永远的盐约（盐即不废坏的意思）。
chapters.en: All the heave offerings of the holy things, which the children of Israel offer unto the LORD, have I given thee, and thy sons and thy daughters with thee, by a statute for ever: it a covenant of salt for ever before the LORD unto thee and to thy seed with thee.

chapters.zh: 耶和华对亚伦说：你在以色列人的境内不可有产业，在他们中间也不可有分。我就是你的分，是你的产业。
chapters.en: And the LORD spake unto Aaron, Thou shalt have no inheritance in their land, neither shalt thou have any part among them: I thy part and thine inheritance among the children of Israel.

chapters.zh: 凡以色列中出产的十分之一，我已赐给利未的子孙为业；因他们所办的是会幕的事，所以赐给他们为酬他们的劳。
chapters.en: And, behold, I have given the children of Levi all the tenth in Israel for an inheritance, for their service which they serve, the service of the tabernacle of the congregation.

chapters.zh: 从今以后，以色列人不可挨近会幕，免得他们担罪而死。
chapters.en: Neither must the children of Israel henceforth come nigh the tabernacle of the congregation, lest they bear sin, and die.

chapters.zh: 惟独利未人要办会幕的事，担当罪孽；这要作你们世世代代永远的定例。他们在以色列人中不可有产业；
chapters.en: But the Levites shall do the service of the tabernacle of the congregation, and they shall bear their iniquity: a statute for ever throughout your generations, that among the children of Israel they have no inheritance.

chapters.zh: 因为以色列人中出产的十分之一，就是献给耶和华为举祭的，我已赐给利未人为业。所以我对他们说：在以色列人中不可有产业。
chapters.en: But the tithes of the children of Israel, which they offer an heave offering unto the LORD, I have given to the Levites to inherit: therefore I have said unto them, Among the children of Israel they shall have no inheritance.

chapters.zh: 耶和华吩咐摩西说：
chapters.en: And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

chapters.zh: 你晓谕利未人说：你们从以色列人中所取的十分之一，就是我给你们为业的，要再从那十分之一中取十分之一作为举祭献给耶和华，
chapters.en: Thus speak unto the Levites, and say unto them, When ye take of the children of Israel the tithes which I have given you from them for your inheritance, then ye shall offer up an heave offering of it for the LORD, a tenth of the tithe.

chapters.zh: 这举祭要算为你们场上的谷，又如满酒醡的酒。
chapters.en: And your heave offering shall be reckoned unto you, as though the corn of the threshingfloor, and as the fulness of the winepress.

chapters.zh: 这样，你们从以色列人中所得十分之一也要作举祭献给耶和华，从这十分之一中，将所献给耶和华的举祭归给祭司亚伦。
chapters.en: Thus ye also shall offer an heave offering unto the LORD of all your tithes, which ye receive of the children of Israel; and ye shall give thereof the LORD'S heave offering to Aaron the priest.

chapters.zh: 奉给你们的一切礼物，要从其中将至好的，就是分别为圣的，献给耶和华为举祭。
chapters.en: Out of all your gifts ye shall offer every heave offering of the LORD, of all the best thereof, the hallowed part thereof out of it.

chapters.zh: 所以你要对利未人说：你们从其中将至好的举起，这就算为你们场上的粮，又如酒醡的酒。
chapters.en: Therefore thou shalt say unto them, When ye have heaved the best thereof from it, then it shall be counted unto the Levites as the increase of the threshingfloor, and as the increase of the winepress.

chapters.zh: 你们和你们家属随处可以吃；这原是你们的赏赐，是酬你们在会幕里办事的劳。
chapters.en: And ye shall eat it in every place, ye and your households: for it your reward for your service in the tabernacle of the congregation.

chapters.zh: 你们从其中将至好的举起，就不至因这物担罪。你们不可亵渎以色列人的圣物，免得死亡。
chapters.en: And ye shall bear no sin by reason of it, when ye have heaved from it the best of it: neither shall ye pollute the holy things of the children of Israel, lest ye die.

chapters.zh: 耶和华晓谕摩西、亚伦说：
chapters.en: And the LORD spake unto Moses and unto Aaron, saying,

chapters.zh: 耶和华命定律法中的一条律例乃是这样说：你要吩咐以色列人，把一只没有残疾、未曾负轭、纯红的母牛牵到你这里来，
chapters.en: This the ordinance of the law which the LORD hath commanded, saying, Speak unto the children of Israel, that they bring thee a red heifer without spot, wherein no blemish, upon which never came yoke:

chapters.zh: 交给祭司以利亚撒；他必牵到营外，人就把牛宰在他面前。
chapters.en: And ye shall give her unto Eleazar the priest, that he may bring her forth without the camp, and shall slay her before his face:

chapters.zh: 祭司以利亚撒要用指头蘸这牛的血，向会幕前面弹七次。
chapters.en: And Eleazar the priest shall take of her blood with his finger, and sprinkle of her blood directly before the tabernacle of the congregation seven times:

chapters.zh: 人要在他眼前把这母牛焚烧；牛的皮、肉、血、粪都要焚烧。
chapters.en: And shall burn the heifer in his sight; her skin, and her flesh, and her blood, with her dung, shall he burn:

chapters.zh: 祭司要把香柏木、牛膝草、朱红色线都丢在烧牛的火中。
chapters.en: And the priest shall take cedar wood, and hyssop, and scarlet, and cast into the midst of the burning of the heifer.

chapters.zh: 祭司必不洁净到晚上，要洗衣服，用水洗身，然后可以进营。
chapters.en: Then the priest shall wash his clothes, and he shall bathe his flesh in water, and afterward he shall come into the camp, and the priest shall be unclean until the even.

chapters.zh: 烧牛的人必不洁净到晚上，也要洗衣服，用水洗身。
chapters.en: And he that burneth her shall wash his clothes in water, and bathe his flesh in water, and shall be unclean until the even.

chapters.zh: 必有一个洁净的人收起母牛的灰，存在营外洁净的地方，为以色列会众调做除污秽的水。这本是除罪的。
chapters.en: And a man clean shall gather up the ashes of the heifer, and lay up without the camp in a clean place, and it shall be kept for the congregation of the children of Israel for a water of separation: it a purification for sin.

chapters.zh: 收起母牛灰的人必不洁净到晚上，要洗衣服。这要给以色列人和寄居在他们中间的外人作为永远的定例。
chapters.en: And he that gathereth the ashes of the heifer shall wash his clothes, and be unclean until the even: and it shall be unto the children of Israel, and unto the stranger that sojourneth among them, for a statute for ever.

chapters.zh: 摸了人死尸的，就必七天不洁净。
chapters.en: He that toucheth the dead body of any man shall be unclean seven days.

chapters.zh: 那人到第三天要用这除污秽的水洁净自己，第七天就洁净了。他若在第三天不洁净自己，第七天就不洁净了。
chapters.en: He shall purify himself with it on the third day, and on the seventh day he shall be clean: but if he purify not himself the third day, then the seventh day he shall not be clean.

chapters.zh: 凡摸了人死尸、不洁净自己的，就玷污了耶和华的帐幕，这人必从以色列中剪除；因为那除污秽的水没有洒在他身上，他就为不洁净，污秽还在他身上。
chapters.en: Whosoever toucheth the dead body of any man that is dead, and purifieth not himself, defileth the tabernacle of the LORD; and that soul shall be cut off from Israel: because the water of separation was not sprinkled upon him, he shall be unclean; his uncleanness yet upon him.

chapters.zh: 人死在帐棚里的条例乃是这样：凡进那帐棚的，和一切在帐棚里的，都必七天不洁净。
chapters.en: This the law, when a man dieth in a tent: all that come into the tent, and all that in the tent, shall be unclean seven days.

chapters.zh: 凡敞口的器皿，就是没有扎上盖的，也是不洁净。
chapters.en: And every open vessel, which hath no covering bound upon it, unclean.

chapters.zh: 无论何人在田野里摸了被刀杀的，或是尸首，或是人的骨头，或是坟墓，就要七天不洁净。
chapters.en: And whosoever toucheth one that is slain with a sword in the open fields, or a dead body, or a bone of a man, or a grave, shall be unclean seven days.

chapters.zh: 要为这不洁净的人拿些烧成的除罪灰放在器皿里，倒上活水。
chapters.en: And for an unclean they shall take of the ashes of the burnt heifer of purification for sin, and running water shall be put thereto in a vessel:

chapters.zh: 必当有一个洁净的人拿牛膝草蘸在这水中，把水洒在帐棚上，和一切器皿并帐棚内的众人身上，又洒在摸了骨头，或摸了被杀的，或摸了自死的，或摸了坟墓的那人身上。
chapters.en: And a clean person shall take hyssop, and dip in the water, and sprinkle upon the tent, and upon all the vessels, and upon the persons that were there, and upon him that touched a bone, or one slain, or one dead, or a grave:

chapters.zh: 第三天和第七天，洁净的人要洒水在不洁净的人身上，第七天就使他成为洁净。那人要洗衣服，用水洗澡，到晚上就洁净了。
chapters.en: And the clean shall sprinkle upon the unclean on the third day, and on the seventh day: and on the seventh day he shall purify himself, and wash his clothes, and bathe himself in water, and shall be clean at even.

chapters.zh: 但那污秽而不洁净自己的，要将他从会中剪除，因为他玷污了耶和华的圣所。除污秽的水没有洒在他身上，他是不洁净的。
chapters.en: But the man that shall be unclean, and shall not purify himself, that soul shall be cut off from among the congregation, because he hath defiled the sanctuary of the LORD: the water of separation hath not been sprinkled upon him; he unclean.

chapters.zh: 这要给你们作为永远的定例。并且那洒除污秽水的人要洗衣服。凡摸除污秽水的，必不洁净到晚上。
chapters.en: And it shall be a perpetual statute unto them, that he that sprinkleth the water of separation shall wash his clothes; and he that toucheth the water of separation shall be unclean until even.

chapters.zh: 不洁净人所摸的一切物就不洁净；摸了这物的人必不洁净到晚上。
chapters.en: And whatsoever the unclean toucheth shall be unclean; and the soul that toucheth shall be unclean until even.

chapters.zh: 正月间，以色列全会众到了寻的旷野，就住在加低斯。米利暗死在那里，就葬在那里。
chapters.en: Then came the children of Israel, the whole congregation, into the desert of Zin in the first month: and the people abode in Kadesh; and Miriam died there, and was buried there.

chapters.zh: 会众没有水喝，就聚集攻击摩西、亚伦。
chapters.en: And there was no water for the congregation: and they gathered themselves together against Moses and against Aaron.

chapters.zh: 百姓向摩西争闹说：我们的弟兄曾死在耶和华面前，我们恨不得与他们同死。
chapters.en: And the people chode with Moses, and spake, saying, Would God that we had died when our brethren died before the LORD!

chapters.zh: 你们为何把耶和华的会众领到这旷野、使我们和牲畜都死在这里呢？
chapters.en: And why have ye brought up the congregation of the LORD into this wilderness, that we and our cattle should die there?

chapters.zh: 你们为何逼著我们出埃及、领我们到这坏地方呢？这地方不好撒种，也没有无花果树、葡萄树、石榴树，又没有水喝。
chapters.en: And wherefore have ye made us to come up out of Egypt, to bring us in unto this evil place? it no place of seed, or of figs, or of vines, or of pomegranates; neither there any water to drink.

chapters.zh: 摩西、亚伦离开会众，到会幕门口，俯伏在地；耶和华的荣光向他们显现。
chapters.en: And Moses and Aaron went from the presence of the assembly unto the door of the tabernacle of the congregation, and they fell upon their faces: and the glory of the LORD appeared unto them.

chapters.zh: 耶和华晓谕摩西说：
chapters.en: And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,

chapters.zh: 你拿著杖去，和你的哥哥亚伦招聚会众，在他们眼前吩咐磐石发出水来，水就从磐石流出，给会众和他们的牲畜喝。
chapters.en: Take the rod, and gather thou the assembly together, thou, and Aaron thy brother, and speak ye unto the rock before their eyes; and it shall give forth his water, and thou shalt bring forth to them water out of the rock: so thou shalt give the congregation and their beasts drink.

chapters.zh: 于是摩西照耶和华所吩咐的，从耶和华面前取了杖去。
chapters.en: And Moses took the rod from before the LORD, as he commanded him.

chapters.zh: 摩西、亚伦就招聚会众到磐石前。摩西说：你们这些背叛的人听我说：我为你们使水从这磐石中流出来么？
chapters.en: And Moses and Aaron gathered the congregation together before the rock, and he said unto them, Hear now, ye rebels; must we fetch you water out of this rock?

chapters.zh: 摩西举手，用杖击打磐石两下，就有许多水流出来，会众和他们的牲畜都喝了。
chapters.en: And Moses lifted up his hand, and with his rod he smote the rock twice: and the water came out abundantly, and the congregation drank, and their beasts .

chapters.zh: 耶和华对摩西、亚伦说：因为你们不信我，不在以色列人眼前尊我为圣，所以你们必不得领这会众进我所赐给他们的地去。
chapters.en: And the LORD spake unto Moses and Aaron, Because ye believed me not, to sanctify me in the eyes of the children of Israel, therefore ye shall not bring this congregation into the land which I have given them.

chapters.zh: 这水名叫米利巴水，是因以色列人向耶和华争闹，耶和华就在他们面前显为圣。（米利巴就是争闹的意思）
chapters.en: This the water of Meribah; because the children of Israel strove with the LORD, and he was sanctified in them.

chapters.zh: 摩西从加低斯差遣使者去见以东王，说：你的弟兄以色列人这样说：我们所遭遇的一切艰难，
chapters.en: And Moses sent messengers from Kadesh unto the king of Edom, Thus saith thy brother Israel, Thou knowest all the travail that hath befallen us:

chapters.zh: 就是我们的列祖下到埃及，我们在埃及久住；埃及人恶待我们的列祖和我们，
chapters.en: How our fathers went down into Egypt, and we have dwelt in Egypt a long time; and the Egyptians vexed us, and our fathers:

chapters.zh: 我们哀求耶和华的时候，他听了我们的声音，差遣使者把我们从埃及领出来。这事你都知道。如今，我们在你边界上的城加低斯。
chapters.en: And when we cried unto the LORD, he heard our voice, and sent an angel, and hath brought us forth out of Egypt: and, behold, we in Kadesh, a city in the uttermost of thy border:

chapters.zh: 求你容我们从你的地经过。我们不走田间和葡萄园，也不喝井里的水，只走大道（原文作王道），不偏左右，直到过了你的境界。
chapters.en: Let us pass, I pray thee, through thy country: we will not pass through the fields, or through the vineyards, neither will we drink the water of the wells: we will go by the king's way, we will not turn to the right hand nor to the left, until we have passed thy borders.

chapters.zh: 以东王说：你不可从我的地经过，免得我带刀出去攻击你。
chapters.en: And Edom said unto him, Thou shalt not pass by me, lest I come out against thee with the sword.

chapters.zh: 以色列人说：我们要走大道上去；我们和牲畜若喝你的水，必给你价值。不求别的，只求你容我们步行过去。
chapters.en: And the children of Israel said unto him, We will go by the high way: and if I and my cattle drink of thy water, then I will pay for it: I will only, without any thing , go through on my feet.

chapters.zh: 以东王说：你们不可经过！就率领许多人出来，要用强硬的手攻击以色列人。
chapters.en: And he said, Thou shalt not go through. And Edom came out against him with much people, and with a strong hand.

chapters.zh: 这样，以东王不肯容以色列人从他的境界过去。于是他们转去，离开他。
chapters.en: Thus Edom refused to give Israel passage through his border: wherefore Israel turned away from him.

chapters.zh: 以色列全会众从加低斯起行，到了何珥山。
chapters.en: And the children of Israel, the whole congregation, journeyed from Kadesh, and came unto mount Hor.

chapters.zh: 耶和华在附近以东边界的何珥山上晓谕摩西、亚伦说：
chapters.en: And the LORD spake unto Moses and Aaron in mount Hor, by the coast of the land of Edom, saying,

chapters.zh: 亚伦要归到他列祖（原文作本民）那里。他必不得入我所赐给以色列人的地；因为在米利巴水，你们违背了我的命。
chapters.en: Aaron shall be gathered unto his people: for he shall not enter into the land which I have given unto the children of Israel, because ye rebelled against my word at the water of Meribah.

chapters.zh: 你带亚伦和他的儿子以利亚撒上何珥山，
chapters.en: Take Aaron and Eleazar his son, and bring them up unto mount Hor:

chapters.zh: 把亚伦的圣衣脱下来，给他的儿子以利亚撒穿上；亚伦必死在那里，归他列祖。
chapters.en: And strip Aaron of his garments, and put them upon Eleazar his son: and Aaron shall be gathered , and shall die there.

chapters.zh: 摩西就照耶和华所吩咐的行。三人当著会众的眼前上了何珥山。
chapters.en: And Moses did as the LORD commanded: and they went up into mount Hor in the sight of all the congregation.

chapters.zh: 摩西把亚伦的圣衣脱下来，给他的儿子以利亚撒穿上，亚伦就死在山顶那里。于是摩西和以利亚撒下了山。
chapters.en: And Moses stripped Aaron of his garments, and put them upon Eleazar his son; and Aaron died there in the top of the mount: and Moses and Eleazar came down from the mount.

chapters.zh: 全会众，就是以色列全家，见亚伦已经死了，便都为亚伦哀哭了三十天。
chapters.en: And when all the congregation saw that Aaron was dead, they mourned for Aaron thirty days, all the house of Israel.

chapters.zh: 住南地的迦南人亚拉得王，听说以色列人从亚他林路来，就和以色列人争战，掳了他们几个人。
chapters.en: And king Arad the Canaanite, which dwelt in the south, heard tell that Israel came by the way of the spies; then he fought against Israel, and took of them prisoners.

chapters.zh: 以色列人向耶和华发愿说：你若将这民交付我手，我就把他们的城邑尽行毁灭。
chapters.en: And Israel vowed a vow unto the LORD, and said, If thou wilt indeed deliver this people into my hand, then I will utterly destroy their cities.

chapters.zh: 耶和华应允了以色列人，把迦南人交付他们，他们就把迦南人和迦南人的城邑尽行毁灭。那地方的名便叫何珥玛（何珥玛就是毁灭的意思）。
chapters.en: And the LORD hearkened to the voice of Israel, and delivered up the Canaanites; and they utterly destroyed them and their cities: and he called the name of the place Hormah.

chapters.zh: 他们从何珥山起行，往红海那条路走，要绕过以东地。百姓因这路难行，心中甚是烦躁，
chapters.en: And they journeyed from mount Hor by the way of the Red sea, to compass the land of Edom: and the soul of the people was much discouraged because of the way.

chapters.zh: 就怨讟神和摩西说：你们为甚么把我们从埃及领出来、使我们死在旷野呢？这里没有粮，没有水，我们的心厌恶这淡薄的食物。
chapters.en: And the people spake against God, and against Moses, Wherefore have ye brought us up out of Egypt to die in the wilderness? for no bread, neither water; and our soul loatheth this light bread.

chapters.zh: 于是耶和华使火蛇进入百姓中间，蛇就咬他们。以色列人中死了许多。
chapters.en: And the LORD sent fiery serpents among the people, and they bit the people; and much people of Israel died.

chapters.zh: 百姓到摩西那里，说：我们怨讟耶和华和你，有罪了。求你祷告耶和华，叫这些蛇离开我们。于是摩西为百姓祷告。
chapters.en: Therefore the people came to Moses, and said, We have sinned, for we have spoken against the LORD, and against thee; pray unto the LORD, that he take away the serpents from us. And Moses prayed for the people.

chapters.zh: 耶和华对摩西说：你制造一条火蛇，挂在杆子上；凡被咬的，一望这蛇，就必得活。
chapters.en: And the LORD said unto Moses, Make thee a fiery serpent, and set it upon a pole: and it shall come to pass, that every one that is bitten, when he looketh upon it, shall live.

chapters.zh: 摩西便制造一条铜蛇，挂在杆子上；凡被蛇咬的，一望这铜蛇就活了。
chapters.en: And Moses made a serpent of brass, and put it upon a pole, and it came to pass, that if a serpent had bitten any man, when he beheld the serpent of brass, he lived.

chapters.zh: 以色列人起行，安营在阿伯。
chapters.en: And the children of Israel set forward, and pitched in Oboth.

chapters.zh: 又从阿伯起行，安营在以耶亚巴琳，与摩押相对的旷野，向日出之地。
chapters.en: And they journeyed from Oboth, and pitched at Ijeabarim, in the wilderness which before Moab, toward the sunrising.

chapters.zh: 从那里起行，安营在撒烈谷。
chapters.en: From thence they removed, and pitched in the valley of Zared.

chapters.zh: 从那里起行，安营在亚嫩河那边。这亚嫩河是在旷野，从亚摩利的境界流出来的；原来亚嫩河是摩押的边界，在摩押和亚摩利人搭界的地方。
chapters.en: From thence they removed, and pitched on the other side of Arnon, which in the wilderness that cometh out of the coasts of the Amorites: for Arnon the border of Moab, between Moab and the Amorites.

chapters.zh: 所以耶和华的战记上说：苏法的哇哈伯与亚嫩河的谷，
chapters.en: Wherefore it is said in the book of the wars of the LORD, What he did in the Red sea, and in the brooks of Arnon,

chapters.zh: 并向亚珥城众谷的下坡，是靠近摩押的境界。
chapters.en: And at the stream of the brooks that goeth down to the dwelling of Ar, and lieth upon the border of Moab.

chapters.zh: 以色列人从那里起行，到了比珥（比珥就是井的意思）。从前耶和华吩咐摩西说：招聚百姓，我好给他们水喝，说的就是这井。
chapters.en: And from thence to Beer: that the well whereof the LORD spake unto Moses, Gather the people together, and I will give them water.

chapters.zh: 当时，以色列人唱歌说：井阿，涌上水来！你们要向这井歌唱。
chapters.en: Then Israel sang this song, Spring up, O well; sing ye unto it:

chapters.zh: 这井是首领和民中的尊贵人用圭用杖所挖所掘的。以色列人从旷野往玛他拿去，
chapters.en: The princes digged the well, the nobles of the people digged it, by the lawgiver, with their staves. And from the wilderness to Mattanah:

chapters.zh: 从玛他拿到拿哈列，从拿哈列到巴末，
chapters.en: And from Mattanah to Nahaliel: and from Nahaliel to Bamoth:

chapters.zh: 从巴末到了摩押地的谷，又到那下望旷野之毘斯迦的山顶。
chapters.en: And from Bamoth the valley, that in the country of Moab, to the top of Pisgah, which looketh toward Jeshimon.

chapters.zh: 以色列人差遣使者去见亚摩利人的王西宏，说：
chapters.en: And Israel sent messengers unto Sihon king of the Amorites, saying,

chapters.zh: 求你容我们从你的地经过；我们不偏入田间和葡萄园，也不喝井里的水，只走大道（原文作王道），直到过了你的境界。
chapters.en: Let me pass through thy land: we will not turn into the fields, or into the vineyards; we will not drink the waters of the well: we will go along by the king's way, until we be past thy borders.

chapters.zh: 西宏不容以色列人从他的境界经过，就招聚他的众民出到旷野，要攻击以色列人，到了雅杂与以色列人争战。
chapters.en: And Sihon would not suffer Israel to pass through his border: but Sihon gathered all his people together, and went out against Israel into the wilderness: and he came to Jahaz, and fought against Israel.

chapters.zh: 以色列人用刀杀了他，得了他的地，从亚嫩河到雅博河，直到亚扪人的境界，因为亚扪人的境界多有坚垒。
chapters.en: And Israel smote him with the edge of the sword, and possessed his land from Arnon unto Jabbok, even unto the children of Ammon: for the border of the children of Ammon strong.

chapters.zh: 以色列人夺取这一切的城邑，也住亚摩利人的城邑，就是希实本与希实本的一切乡村。
chapters.en: And Israel took all these cities: and Israel dwelt in all the cities of the Amorites, in Heshbon, and in all the villages thereof.

chapters.zh: 这希实本是亚摩利王西宏的京城；西宏曾与摩押的先王争战，从他手中夺取了全地，直到亚嫩河。
chapters.en: For Heshbon the city of Sihon the king of the Amorites, who had fought against the former king of Moab, and taken all his land out of his hand, even unto Arnon.

chapters.zh: 所以那些作诗歌的说：你们来到希实本；愿西宏的城被修造，被建立。
chapters.en: Wherefore they that speak in proverbs say, Come into Heshbon, let the city of Sihon be built and prepared:

chapters.zh: 因为有火从希实本发出，有火燄出于西宏的城，烧尽摩押的亚珥和亚嫩河邱坛的祭司（祭司原文作主）。
chapters.en: For there is a fire gone out of Heshbon, a flame from the city of Sihon: it hath consumed Ar of Moab, the lords of the high places of Arnon.

chapters.zh: 摩押阿，你有祸了！基抹的民哪，你们灭亡了！基抹的男子逃奔，女子被掳，交付亚摩利的王西宏。
chapters.en: Woe to thee, Moab! thou art undone, O people of Chemosh: he hath given his sons that escaped, and his daughters, into captivity unto Sihon king of the Amorites.

chapters.zh: 我们射了他们；希实本直到底本尽皆毁灭。我们使地变成荒场，直到挪法；这挪法直延到米底巴。
chapters.en: We have shot at them; Heshbon is perished even unto Dibon, and we have laid them waste even unto Nophah, which unto Medeba.

chapters.zh: 这样，以色列人就住在亚摩利人之地。
chapters.en: Thus Israel dwelt in the land of the Amorites.

chapters.zh: 摩西打发人去窥探雅谢，以色列人就占了雅谢的镇市，赶出那里的亚摩利人。
chapters.en: And Moses sent to spy out Jaazer, and they took the villages thereof, and drove out the Amorites that there.

chapters.zh: 以色列人转回，向巴珊去。巴珊王噩和他的众民都出来，在以得来与他们交战。
chapters.en: And they turned and went up by the way of Bashan: and Og the king of Bashan went out against them, he, and all his people, to the battle at Edrei.

chapters.zh: 耶和华对摩西说：不要怕他！因我已将他和他的众民，并他的地，都交在你手中；你要待他像从前待住希实本的亚摩利王西宏一般。
chapters.en: And the LORD said unto Moses, Fear him not: for I have delivered him into thy hand, and all his people, and his land; and thou shalt do to him as thou didst unto Sihon king of the Amorites, which dwelt at Heshbon.

chapters.zh: 于是他们杀了他和他的众子，并他的众民，没有留下一个，就得了他的地。
chapters.en: So they smote him, and his sons, and all his people, until there was none left him alive: and they possessed his land.

chapters.zh: 以色列人起行，在摩押平原、约但河东，对著耶利哥安营。
chapters.en: And the children of Israel set forward, and pitched in the plains of Moab on this side Jordan Jericho.

chapters.zh: 以色列人向亚摩利人所行的一切事，西拨的儿子巴勒都看见了。
chapters.en: And Balak the son of Zippor saw all that Israel had done to the Amorites.

chapters.zh: 摩押人因以色列民甚多，就大大惧怕，心内忧急，
chapters.en: And Moab was sore afraid of the people, because they many: and Moab was distressed because of the children of Israel.

chapters.zh: 对米甸的长老说：现在这众人要把我们四围所有的一概餂尽，就如牛餂尽田间的草一般。那时西拨的儿子巴勒作摩押王。
chapters.en: And Moab said unto the elders of Midian, Now shall this company lick up all round about us, as the ox licketh up the grass of the field. And Balak the son of Zippor king of the Moabites at that time.

chapters.zh: 他差遣使者往大河边的毘夺去，到比珥的儿子巴兰本乡那里，召巴兰来，说：有一宗民从埃及出来，遮满地面，与我对居。
chapters.en: He sent messengers therefore unto Balaam the son of Beor to Pethor, which by the river of the land of the children of his people, to call him, saying, Behold, there is a people come out from Egypt: behold, they cover the face of the earth, and they abide over against me:

chapters.zh: 这民比我强盛，现在求你来为我咒诅他们，或者我能得胜，攻打他们，赶出此地。因为我知道，你为谁祝福，谁就得福；你咒诅谁，谁就受咒诅。
chapters.en: Come now therefore, I pray thee, curse me this people; for they too mighty for me: peradventure I shall prevail, we may smite them, and I may drive them out of the land: for I wot that he whom thou blessest blessed, and he whom thou cursest is cursed.

chapters.zh: 摩押的长老和米甸的长老手里拿著卦金，到了巴兰那里，将巴勒的话都告诉了他。
chapters.en: And the elders of Moab and the elders of Midian departed with the rewards of divination in their hand; and they came unto Balaam, and spake unto him the words of Balak.

chapters.zh: 巴兰说：你们今夜在这里住宿，我必照耶和华所晓谕我的回报你们。摩押的使臣就在巴兰那里住下了。
chapters.en: And he said unto them, Lodge here this night, and I will bring you word again, as the LORD shall speak unto me: and the princes of Moab abode with Balaam.

chapters.zh: 神临到巴兰那里，说：在你这里的人都是谁？
chapters.en: And God came unto Balaam, and said, What men these with thee?

chapters.zh: 巴兰回答说：是摩押王西拨的儿子巴勒打发人到我这里来，说：
chapters.en: And Balaam said unto God, Balak the son of Zippor, king of Moab, hath sent unto me, ,

chapters.zh: 从埃及出来的民遮满地面，你来为我咒诅他们，或者我能与他们争战，把他们赶出去。
chapters.en: Behold, a people come out of Egypt, which covereth the face of the earth: come now, curse me them; peradventure I shall be able to overcome them, and drive them out.

chapters.zh: 神对巴兰说：你不可同他们去，也不可咒诅那民，因为那民是蒙福的。
chapters.en: And God said unto Balaam, Thou shalt not go with them; thou shalt not curse the people: for they blessed.

chapters.zh: 巴兰早晨起来，对巴勒的使臣说：你们回本地去罢，因为耶和华不容我和你们同去。
chapters.en: And Balaam rose up in the morning, and said unto the princes of Balak, Get you into your land: for the LORD refuseth to give me leave to go with you.

chapters.zh: 摩押的使臣就起来，回巴勒那里去，说：巴兰不肯和我们同来。
chapters.en: And the princes of Moab rose up, and they went unto Balak, and said, Balaam refuseth to come with us.

chapters.zh: 巴勒又差遣使臣，比先前的又多又尊贵。
chapters.en: And Balak sent yet again princes, more, and more honourable than they.

chapters.zh: 他们到了巴兰那里，对他说：西拨的儿子巴勒这样说：求你不容甚么事拦阻你不到我这里来，
chapters.en: And they came to Balaam, and said to him, Thus saith Balak the son of Zippor, Let nothing, I pray thee, hinder thee from coming unto me:

chapters.zh: 因为我必使你得极大的尊荣。你向我要甚么，我就给你甚么；只求你来为我咒诅这民。
chapters.en: For I will promote thee unto very great honour, and I will do whatsoever thou sayest unto me: come therefore, I pray thee, curse me this people.

chapters.zh: 巴兰回答巴勒的臣仆说：巴勒就是将他满屋的金银给我，我行大事小事也不得越过耶和华 ─ 我神的命。
chapters.en: And Balaam answered and said unto the servants of Balak, If Balak would give me his house full of silver and gold, I cannot go beyond the word of the LORD my God, to do less or more.

chapters.zh: 现在我请你们今夜在这里住宿，等我得知耶和华还要对我说甚么。
chapters.en: Now therefore, I pray you, tarry ye also here this night, that I may know what the LORD will say unto me more.

chapters.zh: 当夜，神临到巴兰那里，说：这些人若来召你，你就起来同他们去，你只要遵行我对你所说的话。
chapters.en: And God came unto Balaam at night, and said unto him, If the men come to call thee, rise up, go with them; but yet the word which I shall say unto thee, that shalt thou do.

chapters.zh: 巴兰早晨起来，备上驴，和摩押的使臣一同去了。
chapters.en: And Balaam rose up in the morning, and saddled his ass, and went with the princes of Moab.

chapters.zh: 神因他去就发了怒；耶和华的使者站在路上敌挡他。他骑著驴，有两个仆人跟随他。
chapters.en: And God's anger was kindled because he went: and the angel of the LORD stood in the way for an adversary against him. Now he was riding upon his ass, and his two servants with him.

chapters.zh: 驴看见耶和华的使者站在路上，手里有拔出来的刀，就从路上跨进田间，巴兰便打驴，要叫他回转上路。
chapters.en: And the ass saw the angel of the LORD standing in the way, and his sword drawn in his hand: and the ass turned aside out of the way, and went into the field: and Balaam smote the ass, to turn her into the way.

chapters.zh: 耶和华的使者就站在葡萄园的窄路上；这边有墙，那边也有墙。
chapters.en: But the angel of the LORD stood in a path of the vineyards, a wall on this side, and a wall on that side.

chapters.zh: 驴看见耶和华的使者，就贴靠墙，将巴兰的脚挤伤了；巴兰又打驴。
chapters.en: And when the ass saw the angel of the LORD, she thrust herself unto the wall, and crushed Balaam's foot against the wall: and he smote her again.

chapters.zh: 耶和华的使者又往前去，站在狭窄之处，左右都没有转折的地方。
chapters.en: And the angel of the LORD went further, and stood in a narrow place, where no way to turn either to the right hand or to the left.

chapters.zh: 驴看见耶和华的使者，就卧在巴兰底下，巴兰发怒，用杖打驴。
chapters.en: And when the ass saw the angel of the LORD, she fell down under Balaam: and Balaam's anger was kindled, and he smote the ass with a staff.

chapters.zh: 耶和华叫驴开口，对巴兰说：我向你行了甚么，你竟打我这三次呢？
chapters.en: And the LORD opened the mouth of the ass, and she said unto Balaam, What have I done unto thee, that thou hast smitten me these three times?

chapters.zh: 巴兰对驴说：因为你戏弄我，我恨不能手中有刀，把你杀了。
chapters.en: And Balaam said unto the ass, Because thou hast mocked me: I would there were a sword in mine hand, for now would I kill thee.

chapters.zh: 驴对巴兰说：我不是你从小时直到今日所骑的驴么？我素常向你这样行过么？巴兰说：没有。
chapters.en: And the ass said unto Balaam, not I thine ass, upon which thou hast ridden ever since thine unto this day? was I ever wont to do so unto thee? And he said, Nay.

chapters.zh: 当时，耶和华使巴兰的眼目明亮，他就看见耶和华的使者站在路上，手里有拔出来的刀，巴兰便低头俯伏在地。
chapters.en: Then the LORD opened the eyes of Balaam, and he saw the angel of the LORD standing in the way, and his sword drawn in his hand: and he bowed down his head, and fell flat on his face.

chapters.zh: 耶和华的使者对他说：你为何这三次打你的驴呢？我出来敌挡你，因你所行的，在我面前偏僻。
chapters.en: And the angel of the LORD said unto him, Wherefore hast thou smitten thine ass these three times? behold, I went out to withstand thee, because way is perverse before me:

chapters.zh: 驴看见我就三次从我面前偏过去；驴若没有偏过去，我早把你杀了，留他存活。
chapters.en: And the ass saw me, and turned from me these three times: unless she had turned from me, surely now also I had slain thee, and saved her alive.

chapters.zh: 巴兰对耶和华的使者说：我有罪了。我不知道你站在路上阻挡我；你若不喜欢我去，我就转回。
chapters.en: And Balaam said unto the angel of the LORD, I have sinned; for I knew not that thou stoodest in the way against me: now therefore, if it displease thee, I will get me back again.

chapters.zh: 耶和华的使者对巴兰说：你同这些人去罢！你只要说我对你说的话。于是巴兰同著巴勒的使臣去了。
chapters.en: And the angel of the LORD said unto Balaam, Go with the men: but only the word that I shall speak unto thee, that thou shalt speak. So Balaam went with the princes of Balak.

chapters.zh: 巴勒听见巴兰来了，就往摩押京城去迎接他；这城是在边界上，在亚嫩河旁。
chapters.en: And when Balak heard that Balaam was come, he went out to meet him unto a city of Moab, which in the border of Arnon, which in the utmost coast.

chapters.zh: 巴勒对巴兰说：我不是急急的打发人到你那里去召你么？你为何不到我这里来呢？我岂不能使你得尊荣么？
chapters.en: And Balak said unto Balaam, Did I not earnestly send unto thee to call thee? wherefore camest thou not unto me? am I not able indeed to promote thee to honour?

chapters.zh: 巴兰说：我已经到你这里来了！现在我岂能擅自说甚么呢？神将甚么话传给我，我就说甚么。
chapters.en: And Balaam said unto Balak, Lo, I am come unto thee: have I now any power at all to say any thing? the word that God putteth in my mouth, that shall I speak.

chapters.zh: 巴兰和巴勒同行，来到基列胡琐。
chapters.en: And Balaam went with Balak, and they came unto Kirjathhuzoth.

chapters.zh: 巴勒宰了（原文作献）牛羊，送给巴兰和陪伴的使臣。
chapters.en: And Balak offered oxen and sheep, and sent to Balaam, and to the princes that with him.

chapters.zh: 到了早晨，巴勒领巴兰到巴力的高处；巴兰从那里观看以色列营的边界。
chapters.en: And it came to pass on the morrow, that Balak took Balaam, and brought him up into the high places of Baal, that thence he might see the utmost of the people.

chapters.zh: 巴兰对巴勒说：你在这里给我筑七座坛，为我豫备七只公牛，七只公羊。
chapters.en: And Balaam said unto Balak, Build me here seven altars, and prepare me here seven oxen and seven rams.

chapters.zh: 巴勒照巴兰的话行了。巴勒和巴兰在每座坛上献一只公牛，一只公羊。
chapters.en: And Balak did as Balaam had spoken; and Balak and Balaam offered on altar a bullock and a ram.

chapters.zh: 巴兰对巴勒说：你站在你的燔祭旁边，我且往前去，或者耶和华来迎见我。他指示我甚么，我必告诉你。于是巴兰上一净光的高处。
chapters.en: And Balaam said unto Balak, Stand by thy burnt offering, and I will go: peradventure the LORD will come to meet me: and whatsoever he sheweth me I will tell thee. And he went to an high place.

chapters.zh: 神迎见巴兰；巴兰说：我豫备了七座坛，在每座坛上献了一只公牛，一只公羊。
